criteria.label: Write PMFs for Bernoulli, Binomial, and Poisson and identify parameters.
criteria.example: State P(X=k) for Binomial(n,p) and Poisson(λ), and label p, n, λ correctly in a word problem about 8 successes in 20 trials or 3 calls with λ=4.

criteria.label: Distinguish PMF from PDF and compute a simple interval probability for Normal data.
criteria.example: Explain why P(X=2)=0 for continuous X, then use a z-score or `stats.norm.cdf(1.5) - stats.norm.cdf(-1.5)` for N(0,1) to get P(−1.5 ≤ X ≤ 1.5).

criteria.label: Map ML problem types to the appropriate distribution and loss.
criteria.example: Given spam detection, daily ad clicks, and MNIST digits, assign Bernoulli+log loss, Poisson or Binomial count likelihood, and Categorical+softmax cross-entropy respectively with one-sentence justification each.

criteria.label: Evaluate distribution assumptions on real or synthetic data.
criteria.example: On a count column, compute mean and variance; if variance ≫ mean, recommend Negative Binomial over Poisson and note the equidispersion failure.

criteria.label: Implement and interpret SciPy or sklearn distribution-based models.
criteria.example: Compute `stats.binom.pmf(7, 20, 0.4)`, fit `GaussianNB` on Iris, and report how class-conditional means θ_ relate to Normal PDFs in the decision rule.
